cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. So the question is asking what we can learn from the passage. The story is about a farmer in central China who has a horse that dies, then gets a new horse, which runs away, comes back, then his son breaks his leg, but then the army doesn't recruit him because of the broken leg. The farmer keeps saying "We'll see" each time people react to the events. The options are A to D. Let me break it down. First, the passage starts by mentioning the farmer is poor and uses an old horse instead of a tractor. That's about his economic status. So option C says "the farmer's family was poor." The passage does say he didn't have money. So that's a fact stated in the story. But the question is asking what we can learn from the passage. The main lesson is about not overreacting and staying calm, as mentioned in the last paragraph. But the options are about specific details or lessons. Option A says the farmer wanted more help from other villages. But in the story, when the horse dies, people in the village give him a new horse because they admire his calmness. It doesn't say he wanted more help; it was a gift from the villagers. So A is probably not correct. Option B claims he was well educated and knowledgeable. The story doesn't mention his education. It's more about his attitude and how he responds to events, not his education level. So B might not be right. Option
